design_note: Recursive deep-merge for nested config dicts. The buggy implementation uses dict.update (shallow merge), so an override at depth 2+ completely replaces the base dict at that key instead of merging into it. The naive level-1 fix (handle nested dicts but don't recurse) ceiling-fails at depth 3. The correct fix requires recursion AND careful edge handling: lists fully REPLACE (not merge into); scalar-vs-dict override at the same key REPLACES based on override's type; equal-type non-dict values just replace. Multiple semantic edges all caught by separate tests so naive single-rule fixes fail. Contract surface deliberately neutral (function called `merge`, no `deep_merge` telegraph; docstring describes behavior in terms of input/output examples rather than naming the algorithm).
